v.CNV: 耶稣上了船，过到自己的城来。
v.ESV: And getting into a boat he crossed over and came to his own city.
v.GAE: 예수께서 배에 오르사 건너가 본 동네에 이르시니
v.KJV: And he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.
v.NASB: Getting into a boat, Jesus crossed over the sea and came to His own city.
v.NIV: Jesus stepped into a boat, crossed over and came to his own town.
v.NRSV: And after getting into a boat he crossed the sea and came to his own town.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 배에 오르셔서, 바다를 건너 자기 마을에 돌아오셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 배를 타고 호수를 건너 자기 마을로 돌아오셨습니다.

v.CNV: 有人把一个躺在床上的瘫子带到他那里。耶稣看见他们的信心，就对瘫子说：“孩子，放心！你的罪赦了。”
v.ESV: And behold, some people brought to him a paralytic, lying on a bed. And when Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, my son; your sins are forgiven.”
v.GAE: 침상에 누운 중풍병자를 사람들이 데리고 오거늘 예수께서 그들의 믿음을 보시고 중풍병자에게 이르시되 작은 자야 안심하라 네 죄 사함을 받았느니라
v.KJV: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.
v.NASB: And they brought to Him a paralytic lying on a bed. Seeing their faith, Jesus said to the paralytic, 'Take courage, son; your sins are forgiven.'
v.NIV: Some men brought to him a paralyzed man, lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man, “Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.”
v.NRSV: And just then some people were carrying a paralyzed man lying on a bed. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.”
v.SAENEW: 사람들이 중풍병 환자 한 사람을, 침상에 누인 채로, 예수께로 날라 왔다. 예수께서 그들의 믿음을 보시고, 중풍병 환자에게 말씀하셨다. "기운을 내라, 아이야. 네 죄가 용서받았다."
v.WLB: 사람들이 중풍 환자 한 사람을 자리에 눕힌 채 예수께 데려왔습니다. 예수께서는 그들의 믿음을 보시고 중풍 환자에게 말씀하셨습니다. “얘야, 안심하여라. 네 죄가 용서받았다.”

v.CNV: 有几位经学家彼此说：“这个人在说僭妄的话。”
v.ESV: And behold, some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
v.GAE: 어떤 서기관들이 속으로 이르되 이 사람이 신성을 모독하도다
v.KJV: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
v.NASB: And some of the scribes said to themselves, 'This fellow blasphemes.'
v.NIV: At this, some of the teachers of the law said to themselves, “This fellow is blaspheming!”
v.NRSV: Then some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
v.SAENEW: 그런데 율법학자 몇이 '이 사람이 하나님을 모독하는구나' 하고 속으로 말하였다.
v.WLB: 이것을 보고 몇몇 율법학자들이 속으로 말했습니다. ‘저 사람이 하나님을 모독하고 있구나.’

v.CNV: 耶稣看出他们所想的，就说：“你们心里为什么存着恶念呢？
v.ESV: But Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
v.GAE: 예수께서 그 생각을 아시고 이르시되 너희가 어찌하여 마음에 악한 생각을 하느냐
v.KJV: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
v.NASB: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, 'Why are you thinking evil in your hearts?
v.NIV: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said, “Why do you entertain evil thoughts in your hearts?
v.NRSV: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들의 생각을 아시고 말씀하셨다. "어찌하여 너희는 마음 속에 악한 생각을 품고 있느냐?
v.WLB: 예수께서 그 생각을 알고 말씀하셨습니다. “왜 그런 악한 생각을 품고 있느냐?

v.CNV: 说‘你的罪赦了’，或说‘起来行走’，哪一样容易呢？
v.ESV: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise and walk’?
v.GAE: 네 죄 사함을 받았느니라 하는 말과 일어나 걸어가라 하는 말 중에 어느 것이 쉽겠느냐
v.KJV: For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?
v.NASB: 'Which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up, and walk '?
v.NIV: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
v.NRSV: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and walk’?
v.SAENEW: '네 죄가 용서받았다' 하고 말하는 것과 '일어나서 걸어가거라' 하고 말하는 것 가운데서, 어느 쪽이 더 말하기가 쉬우냐?
v.WLB: ‘네 죄가 용서받았다’는 말과 ‘일어나 걸어가라’는 말 중에 어떤 말이 더 쉽겠느냐?

v.CNV: 然而为了要使你们知道人子在地上有赦罪的权柄，（他就对瘫子说：）起来，拿起你的床，回家去吧。”
v.ESV: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” —he then said to the paralytic— “Rise, pick up your bed and go home.”
v.GAE: 그러나 인자가 세상에서 죄를 사하는 권능이 있는 줄을 너희로 알게 하려 하노라 하시고 중풍병자에게 말씀하시되 일어나 네 침상을 가지고 집으로 가라 하시니
v.KJV: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
v.NASB: 'But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins '-- then He said to the paralytic, 'Get up, pick up your bed and go home.'
v.NIV: But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.” So he said to the paralyzed man, “Get up, take your mat and go home.”
v.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he then said to the paralytic — “Stand up, take your bed and go to your home.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 인자가 땅에서 죄를 용서하는 권세를 가지고 있음을 너희들이 알게 하겠다." 그리고 예수께서 중풍병 환자에게 "일어나서, 네 침상을 거두어 가지고 집으로 가거라" 하시니,
v.WLB: 그러나 인자가 땅에서 죄를 용서하는 권세를 가지고 있음을 너희에게 알려 주겠다.” 그리고 예수께서 중풍 환자에게 말씀하셨습니다. “일어나 네 자리를 들고 집으로 가거라.”

v.CNV: 他就起来回家去了。
v.ESV: And he rose and went home.
v.GAE: 그가 일어나 집으로 돌아가거늘
v.KJV: And he arose, and departed to his house.
v.NASB: And he got up and went home.
v.NIV: Then the man got up and went home.
v.NRSV: And he stood up and went to his home.
v.SAENEW: 그가 일어나서, 자기 집으로 돌아갔다.
v.WLB: 그러자 그 사람은 일어나 집으로 돌아갔습니다.

v.CNV: 群众看见，就起了敬畏的心，颂赞那把这样的权柄赐给人的　神。
v.ESV: When the crowds saw it, they were afraid, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
v.GAE: 무리가 보고 두려워하며 이런 권능을 사람에게 주신 하나님께 영광을 돌리니라
v.KJV: But when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.
v.NASB: But when the crowds saw this, they were awestruck, and glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
v.NIV: When the crowd saw this, they were filled with awe; and they praised God, who had given such authority to man.
v.NRSV: When the crowds saw it, they were filled with awe, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to human beings.
v.SAENEW: 무리가 이 일을 보고서, 두려움에 사로잡히고, 이런 권한을 사람들에게 주신 하나님께 영광을 돌렸다.
v.WLB: 이를 보고 무리는 두려워하며 이런 권세를 사람에게 주신 하나님께 영광을 돌렸습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣从那里往前走，看见一个人，名叫马太，坐在税关那里，就对他说：“来跟从我！”他就起来跟从了耶稣。
v.ESV: As Jesus passed on from there, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he rose and followed him.
v.GAE: 예수께서 그 곳을 떠나 지나가시다가 마태라 하는 사람이 세관에 앉아 있는 것을 보시고 이르시되 나를 따르라 하시니 일어나 따르니라
v.KJV: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
v.NASB: As Jesus went on from there, He saw a man called Matthew, sitting in the tax collector's booth; and He said to him, 'Follow Me!' And he got up and followed Him.
v.NIV: As Jesus went on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax collector’s booth. “Follow me,” he told him, and Matthew got up and followed him.
v.NRSV: As Jesus was walking along, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth; and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 거기에서 떠나서 길을 가시다가, 마태라는 사람이 세관에 앉아 있는 것을 보시고 말씀하셨다. "나를 따라오너라." 그는 일어나서, 예수를 따라갔다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그곳을 떠나 길을 가시다가 마태라는 사람이 세관에 앉아 있는 것을 보셨습니다. 예수께서는 마태에게 “나를 따라라!”고 말씀하셨습니다. 그러자 마태가 일어나 예수를 따랐습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣在屋里吃饭的时候，有很多税吏和罪人来与他和门徒一起吃饭。
v.ESV: And as Jesus reclined at table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were reclining with Jesus and his disciples.
v.GAE: 예수께서 마태의 집에서 앉아 음식을 잡수실 때에 많은 세리와 죄인들이 와서 예수와 그의 제자들과 함께 앉았더니
v.KJV: And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
v.NASB: Then it happened that as Jesus was reclining at the table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were dining with Jesus and His disciples.
v.NIV: While Jesus was having dinner at Matthew’s house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with him and his disciples.
v.NRSV: And as he sat at dinner in the house, many tax collectors and sinners came and were sitting with him and his disciples.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 집에서 음식을 드시는데, 많은 세리와 죄인이 와서, 예수와 그 제자들과 자리를 같이 하였다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 집에서 저녁을 잡수실 때에 많은 세리와 죄인들도 와서 예수와 그 제자들과 함께 음식을 먹었습니다.

v.CNV: 法利赛人看见了，就对他的门徒说：“你们的老师为什么跟税吏和罪人一起吃饭呢？”
v.ESV: And when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
v.GAE: 바리새인들이 보고 그의 제자들에게 이르되 어찌하여 너희 선생은 세리와 죄인들과 함께 잡수시느냐
v.KJV: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
v.NASB: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to His disciples, 'Why is your Teacher eating with the tax collectors and sinners?'
v.NIV: When the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
v.NRSV: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
v.SAENEW: 바리새파 사람들이 이것을 보고, 예수의 제자들에게 말하였다. "어찌하여 당신네 선생은 세리와 죄인과 어울려서 음식을 드시오?"
v.WLB: 이것을 본 바리새파 사람들이 예수의 제자들에게 물었습니다. “어째서 너희 선생님은 세리들과 죄인들과 함께 어울려 먹느냐?”

v.CNV: 耶稣听见了，就说：“健康的人不需要医生，有病的人才需要，
v.ESV: But when he heard it, he said, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
v.GAE: 예수께서 들으시고 이르시되 건강한 자에게는 의사가 쓸 데 없고 병든 자에게라야 쓸 데 있느니라
v.KJV: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.
v.NASB: But when Jesus heard this, He said, 'It is not those who are healthy who need a physician, but those who are sick.
v.NIV: On hearing this, Jesus said, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.
v.NRSV: But when he heard this, he said, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그 말을 들으시고서 말씀하셨다. "건강한 사람에게는 의사가 필요하지 않으나, 병든 사람에게는 필요하다.
v.WLB: 이 말을 듣고 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “건강한 사람에게는 의사가 필요하지 않으나 병든 사람에게는 의사가 필요하다.

v.CNV: ‘我喜爱怜悯，不喜爱祭祀’，你们去想一想这话的意思吧。我来不是要召义人，而是要召罪人。”
v.ESV: Go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice.’ For I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.”
v.GAE: 너희는 가서 내가 긍휼을 원하고 제사를 원하지 아니하노라 하신 뜻이 무엇인지 배우라 나는 의인을 부르러 온 것이 아니요 죄인을 부르러 왔노라 하시니라
v.KJV: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
v.NASB: 'But go and learn what this means: 'I DESIRE COMPASSION, AND NOT SACRIFICE,' for I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners.'
v.NIV: But go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
v.NRSV: Go and learn what this means, ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
v.SAENEW: 너희는 가서 '내가 바라는 것은 자비요, 희생제물이 아니다' 하신 말씀이 무슨 뜻인지 배워라. 나는 의인을 부르러 온 것이 아니라, 죄인을 부르러 왔다."
v.WLB: 너희는 가서 ‘내가 원하는 것은 제사가 아니라 자비다’ 하신 말씀이 무슨 뜻인지 배워라. 나는 의인을 부르러 온 것이 아니라 죄인을 부르러 왔다.”

v.CNV: 那时，约翰的门徒前来问耶稣：“为什么我们和法利赛人常常禁食，你的门徒却不禁食呢？”
v.ESV: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
v.GAE: 그 때에 요한의 제자들이 예수께 나아와 이르되 우리와 바리새인들은 금식하는데 어찌하여 당신의 제자들은 금식하지 아니하나이까
v.KJV: Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
v.NASB: Then the disciples of John came to Him, asking, 'Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but Your disciples do not fast?'
v.NIV: Then John’s disciples came and asked him, “How is it that we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?”
v.NRSV: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 요한의 제자들이 예수께 와서 물었다. "우리와 바리새파 사람은 자주 금식을 하는데, 왜 선생님의 제자들은 금식을 하지 않습니까?"
v.WLB: 그때 요한의 제자들이 예수께 와서 물었습니다. “우리와 바리새파 사람들은 금식을 하는데 왜 당신의 제자들은 금식하지 않습니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣回答：“新郎跟宾客在一起的时候，宾客怎能哀痛呢？但到了时候，新郎就要从他们中间被取去，那时他们就要禁食了。
v.ESV: And Jesus said to them, “Can the wedding guests mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들에게 이르시되 혼인집 손님들이 신랑과 함께 있을 동안에 슬퍼할 수 있느냐 그러나 신랑을 빼앗길 날이 이르리니 그 때에는 금식할 것이니라
v.KJV: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.
v.NASB: And Jesus said to them, 'The attendants of the bridegroom cannot mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them, can they? But the days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
v.NIV: Jesus answered, “How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while he is with them? The time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.
v.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them, can they? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "혼인 잔치의 손님들이 신랑이 자기들과 함께 있는 동안에 슬퍼할 수 있느냐? 그러나 신랑을 빼앗길 날이 올 터이니, 그 때에는 그들이 금식할 것이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “신랑이 함께 있는데 어떻게 결혼에 초대받은 사람들이 슬퍼할 수 있겠느냐? 그러나 신랑을 빼앗길 날이 올 것이다. 그때에는 그들도 금식할 것이다.

v.CNV: 没有人会拿一块新布补在旧衣服上，因为补上的会把衣服扯破，裂的地方就更大了。
v.ESV: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch tears away from the garment, and a worse tear is made.
v.GAE: 생베 조각을 낡은 옷에 붙이는 자가 없나니 이는 기운 것이 그 옷을 당기어 해어짐이 더하게 됨이요
v.KJV: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
v.NASB: 'But no one puts a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; for the patch pulls away from the garment, and a worse tear results.
v.NIV: “No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch will pull away from the garment, making the tear worse.
v.NRSV: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak, for the patch pulls away from the cloak, and a worse tear is made.
v.SAENEW: 생베 조각을 낡은 옷에다 대고 깁는 사람은 없다. 그렇게 하면, 새로 댄 조각이 그 옷을 당겨서, 더욱더 크게 찢어진다.
v.WLB: 낡은 옷에 새로운 천 조각을 대고 깁는 사람은 없다. 그렇게 하면 새로운 천 조각이 낡은 옷을 잡아당겨 더 찢어지게 된다.

v.CNV: 也没有人会把新酒装在旧皮袋里，如果这样，皮袋就会胀破，酒就漏出来，皮袋也损坏了。人总是把新酒装在新皮袋里，这样，两样都可以保全。”
v.ESV: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins. If it is, the skins burst and the wine is spilled and the skins are destroyed. But new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
v.GAE: 새 포도주를 낡은 가죽 부대에 넣지 아니하나니 그렇게 하면 부대가 터져 포도주도 쏟아지고 부대도 버리게 됨이라 새 포도주는 새 부대에 넣어야 둘이 다 보전되느니라
v.KJV: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.
v.NASB: 'Nor do people put new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the wineskins burst, and the wine pours out and the wineskins are ruined; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.'
v.NIV: Neither do people pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst; the wine will run out and the wineskins will be ruined. No, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.”
v.NRSV: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins; otherwise, the skins burst, and the wine is spilled, and the skins are destroyed; but new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
v.SAENEW: 새 포도주를 낡은 가죽 부대에 담는 사람은 없다. 그렇게 하면, 가죽 부대가 터져서, 포도주는 쏟아지고, 가죽 부대는 못 쓰게 된다. 새 포도주는 새 가죽 부대에 담아야 둘 다 보존된다."
v.WLB: 새 포도주를 낡은 가죽부대에 담는 사람도 없다. 그렇게 하면 부대가 터져 포도주가 쏟아지고 부대도 못 쓰게 된다. 그러니 새 포도주는 새 부대에 담아야 둘 다 보전된다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说话的时候，有一位会堂的主管走来跪在他面前，说：“我的女儿刚死了，但请你来按手在她身上，她必活过来。”
v.ESV: While he was saying these things to them, behold, a ruler came in and knelt before him, saying, “My daughter has just died, but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이 말씀을 하실 때에 한 관리가 와서 절하며 이르되 내 딸이 방금 죽었사오나 오셔서 그 몸에 손을 얹어 주소서 그러면 살아나겠나이다 하니
v.KJV: While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.
v.NASB: While He was saying these things to them, a synagogue official came and bowed down before Him, and said, 'My daughter has just died; but come and lay Your hand on her, and she will live.'
v.NIV: While he was saying this, a synagogue leader came and knelt before him and said, “My daughter has just died. But come and put your hand on her, and she will live.”
v.NRSV: While he was saying these things to them, suddenly a leader of the synagogue came in and knelt before him, saying, “My daughter has just died; but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 이 말씀을 하실 때에, 지도자 한 사람이 와서, 예수께 무릎을 꿇고 말하였다. "내 딸이 방금 죽었습니다. 그러나 오셔서, 그 아이에게 손을 얹어 주십시오. 그러면 살아날 것입니다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 이 말씀을 하고 계실 때에 갑자기 회당장 한 사람이 와서 예수 앞에 무릎을 꿇고 말했습니다. “제 딸이 방금 죽었습니다. 그러나 오셔서 아이에게 손을 얹어 주시면 다시 살아날 것입니다.”

v.CNV: 于是耶稣和门徒起来跟着他去了。
v.ESV: And Jesus rose and followed him, with his disciples.
v.GAE: 예수께서 일어나 따라가시매 제자들도 가더니
v.KJV: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
v.NASB: Jesus got up and began to follow him, and so did His disciples.
v.NIV: Jesus got up and went with him, and so did his disciples.
v.NRSV: And Jesus got up and followed him, with his disciples.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 일어나서 그를 따라가셨고, 제자들도 뒤따라갔다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 일어나 그와 함께 가셨습니다. 제자들도 뒤따랐습니다.

v.CNV: 有一个女人，患了十二年的血漏病，她走到耶稣背后，摸他衣服的繸子，
v.ESV: And behold, a woman who had suffered from a discharge of blood for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his garment,
v.GAE: 열두 해 동안이나 혈루증으로 앓는 여자가 예수의 뒤로 와서 그 겉옷 가를 만지니
v.KJV: And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:
v.NASB: And a woman who had been suffering from a hemorrhage for twelve years, came up behind Him and touched the fringe of His cloak;
v.NIV: Just then a woman who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years came up behind him and touched the edge of his cloak.
v.NRSV: Then suddenly a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his cloak,
v.SAENEW: 그런데 열두 해 동안 혈루증으로 앓는 여자가 뒤에서 예수께로 다가와서, 예수의 옷술에 손을 대었다.
v.WLB: 바로 그때, 12년 동안 혈루병을 앓고 있던 한 여인이 예수 뒤로 다가와 예수의 옷자락을 만졌습니다.

v.CNV: 因为她心里说：“只要摸到他的衣服，我就必痊愈。”
v.ESV: for she said to herself, “If I only touch his garment, I will be made well.”
v.GAE: 이는 제 마음에 그 겉옷만 만져도 구원을 받겠다 함이라
v.KJV: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
v.NASB: for she was saying to herself, 'If I only touch His garment, I will get well.'
v.NIV: She said to herself, “If I only touch his cloak, I will be healed.”
v.NRSV: for she said to herself, “If I only touch his cloak, I will be made well.”
v.SAENEW: 그 여자는 속으로 말하기를 "내가 그의 옷에 손을 대기만 하여도 나을 텐데!" 했던 것이다.
v.WLB: 여인은 ‘예수의 옷자락만 만져도 내가 나을 것이다’라고 생각한 것입니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣转过来看见她，就说：“女儿，放心！你的信心使你痊愈了。”从那时起，那女人就好了。
v.ESV: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And instantly the woman was made well.
v.GAE: 예수께서 돌이켜 그를 보시며 이르시되 딸아 안심하라 네 믿음이 너를 구원하였다 하시니 여자가 그 즉시 구원을 받으니라
v.KJV: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
v.NASB: But Jesus turning and seeing her said, 'Daughter, take courage; your faith has made you well.'
v.NIV: Jesus turned and saw her. “Take heart, daughter,” he said, “your faith has healed you.” And the woman was healed at that moment.
v.NRSV: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And instantly the woman was made well.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 돌아서서, 그 여자를 보시고 말씀하셨다. "기운을 내어라, 딸아. 네 믿음이 너를 구원하였다." 바로 그 때에 그 여자가 나았다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 돌아서서 그 여인을 보시며 말씀하셨습니다. “딸아, 안심하여라. 네 믿음이 너를 구원했다.” 그 순간 여인은 병이 나았습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣进了那主管的家，看见有吹笛的人和喧哗的群众，
v.ESV: And when Jesus came to the ruler's house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
v.GAE: 예수께서 그 관리의 집에 가사 피리 부는 자들과 떠드는 무리를 보시고
v.KJV: And when Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
v.NASB: When Jesus came into the official's house, and saw the flute-players and the crowd in noisy disorder,
v.NIV: When Jesus entered the synagogue leader’s house and saw the noisy crowd and people playing pipes,
v.NRSV: When Jesus came to the leader’s house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그 지도자의 집에 이르러서, 피리를 부는 사람들과 떠드는 무리를 보시고,
v.WLB: 예수께서 그 회당장의 집으로 들어가셨습니다. 그곳에서 피리 부는 사람들과 곡하는 사람들을 보시고

v.CNV: 就说：“出去！这女孩不是死了，只是睡了。”他们就嘲笑他。
v.ESV: he said, “Go away, for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.
v.GAE: 이르시되 물러가라 이 소녀가 죽은 것이 아니라 잔다 하시니 그들이 비웃더라
v.KJV: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.
v.NASB: He said, 'Leave; for the girl has not died, but is asleep.' And they began laughing at Him.
v.NIV: he said, “Go away. The girl is not dead but asleep.” But they laughed at him.
v.NRSV: he said, “Go away; for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 말씀하셨다. "모두 물러가거라. 그 소녀는 죽은 것이 아니라, 자고 있다." 그들은 예수를 비웃었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “물러가라. 이 소녀는 죽은 것이 아니라 자고 있는 것이다.” 그러자 사람들이 비웃었습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣赶走众人之后，进去拉着女孩的手，女孩就起来了。
v.ESV: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl arose.
v.GAE: 무리를 내보낸 후에 예수께서 들어가사 소녀의 손을 잡으시매 일어나는지라
v.KJV: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
v.NASB: But when the crowd had been sent out, He entered and took her by the hand, and the girl got up.
v.NIV: After the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took the girl by the hand, and she got up.
v.NRSV: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl got up.
v.SAENEW: 무리를 내보낸 다음에, 예수께서 들어가셔서, 그 소녀의 손을 잡으시니, 그 소녀가 벌떡 일어났다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 사람들을 집 밖으로 내보내시고 방으로 들어가 소녀의 손을 잡으셨습니다. 그러자 소녀가 일어났습니다.

v.CNV: 这消息传遍了那一带。
v.ESV: And the report of this went through all that district.
v.GAE: 그 소문이 그 온 땅에 퍼지더라
v.KJV: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
v.NASB: This news spread throughout all that land.
v.NIV: News of this spread through all that region.
v.NRSV: And the report of this spread throughout that district.
v.SAENEW: 이 소문이 그 온 땅에 퍼졌다.
v.WLB: 그리고 이 소문은 그 지역 온 사방으로 널리 퍼졌습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣离开那里的时候，有两个瞎子跟着他，喊着说：“大卫的子孙，可怜我们吧！”
v.ESV: And as Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed him, crying aloud, “Have mercy on us, Son of David.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 거기에서 떠나가실새 두 맹인이 따라오며 소리 질러 이르되 다윗의 자손이여 우리를 불쌍히 여기소서 하더니
v.KJV: And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou Son of David, have mercy on us.
v.NASB: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out, 'Have mercy on us, Son of David!'
v.NIV: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, calling out, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
v.NRSV: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, crying loudly, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 거기에서 떠나가시는데, 눈 먼 사람 둘이 "다윗의 자손이여, 우리를 불쌍히 여겨 주십시오" 하고 외치면서 예수를 뒤따라 왔다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그곳을 떠나 길을 가시는데 눈먼 두 사람이 예수를 따라오면서 소리 질렀습니다. “다윗의 자손이여, 우리에게 자비를 베풀어 주소서!”

v.CNV: 耶稣进了房子，他们来到他那里。耶稣问他们：“你们信我能作这些事吗？”他们回答：“主啊，我们信。”
v.ESV: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 집에 들어가시매 맹인들이 그에게 나아오거늘 예수께서 이르시되 내가 능히 이 일 할 줄을 믿느냐 대답하되 주여 그러하오이다 하니
v.KJV: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
v.NASB: When He entered the house, the blind men came up to Him, and Jesus said to them, 'Do you believe that I am able to do this?' They said to Him, 'Yes, Lord.'
v.NIV: When he had gone indoors, the blind men came to him, and he asked them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” “Yes, Lord,” they replied.
v.NRSV: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him; and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 집 안으로 들어가셨는데, 그 눈 먼 사람들이 그에게 나아왔다. 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "너희는 내가 이 일을 할 수 있다고 믿느냐?" 그들이 "예, 주님!" 하고 대답하였다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 어떤 집으로 들어가시자 눈먼 사람들도 따라 들어갔습니다. 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨습니다. “내가 너희를 보게 할 수 있다고 믿느냐?” 그들이 예수께 대답했습니다. “그렇습니다. 주님!”

v.CNV: 于是耶稣摸他们的眼睛，说：“照你们的信心给你们成就吧。”
v.ESV: Then he touched their eyes, saying, “According to your faith let it be done to you.”
v.GAE: 이에 예수께서 그들의 눈을 만지시며 이르시되 너희 믿음대로 되라 하시니
v.KJV: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
v.NASB: Then He touched their eyes, saying, 'It shall be done to you according to your faith.'
v.NIV: Then he touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith let it be done to you”;
v.NRSV: Then he touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith let it be done to you.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들의 눈에 손을 대시고 말씀하셨다. "너희 믿음대로 되어라."
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서는 그들의 눈을 만지시며 말씀하셨습니다. “너희 믿음대로 되라.”

v.CNV: 他们的眼睛就看见了。耶稣严严地嘱咐他们：“千万不可让人知道。”
v.ESV: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them, “See that no one knows about it.”
v.GAE: 그 눈들이 밝아진지라 예수께서 엄히 경고하시되 삼가 아무에게도 알리지 말라 하셨으나
v.KJV: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
v.NASB: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them: 'See that no one knows about this!'
v.NIV: and their sight was restored. Jesus warned them sternly, “See that no one knows about this.”
v.NRSV: And their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly ordered them, “See that no one knows of this.”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 그들의 눈이 열렸다. 예수께서 그들에게 엄중히 다짐하셨다. "이 일을 아무에게도 알리지 말아라."
v.WLB: 그러자 그들의 눈이 열렸습니다. 예수께서는 엄히 당부하셨습니다. “이 일을 아무도 모르게 하라.”

v.CNV: 他们却出去，把他所作的事传遍了那一带。
v.ESV: But they went away and spread his fame through all that district.
v.GAE: 그들이 나가서 예수의 소문을 그 온 땅에 퍼뜨리니라
v.KJV: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.
v.NASB: But they went out and spread the news about Him throughout all that land.
v.NIV: But they went out and spread the news about him all over that region.
v.NRSV: But they went away and spread the news about him throughout that district.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그들은 나가서, 예수의 소문을 그 온 지역에 퍼뜨렸다.
v.WLB: 그러나 그들은 나가서 예수께 대한 소문을 그 지역 온 사방에 퍼뜨렸습니다.

v.CNV: 他们出去的时候，有人带着一个被鬼附着的哑巴来见耶稣。
v.ESV: As they were going away, behold, a demon-oppressed man who was mute was brought to him.
v.GAE: 그들이 나갈 때에 귀신 들려 말 못하는 사람을 예수께 데려오니
v.KJV: As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.
v.NASB: As they were going out, a mute, demon-possessed man was brought to Him.
v.NIV: While they were going out, a man who was demon-possessed and could not talk was brought to Jesus.
v.NRSV: After they had gone away, a demoniac who was mute was brought to him.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 떠나간 뒤에, 귀신이 들려 말 못하는 한 사람을 사람들이 예수께 데리고 왔다.
v.WLB: 그들이 떠나간 뒤 사람들이 귀신 들려 말 못하는 사람을 예수께 데려왔습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣把鬼赶走之后，哑巴就说话了。众人都很惊奇，说：“这样的事，在以色列从来没有见过。”
v.ESV: And when the demon had been cast out, the mute man spoke. And the crowds marveled, saying, “Never was anything like this seen in Israel.”
v.GAE: 귀신이 쫓겨나고 말 못하는 사람이 말하거늘 무리가 놀랍게 여겨 이르되 이스라엘 가운데서 이런 일을 본 적이 없다 하되
v.KJV: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
v.NASB: After the demon was cast out, the mute man spoke; and the crowds were amazed, and were saying, 'Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.'
v.NIV: And when the demon was driven out, the man who had been mute spoke. The crowd was amazed and said, “Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.”
v.NRSV: And when the demon had been cast out, the one who had been mute spoke; and the crowds were amazed and said, “Never has anything like this been seen in Israel.”
v.SAENEW: 귀신이 쫓겨나니, 말 못하는 그 사람이 말을 하게 되었다. 무리가 놀라서 말하였다. "이런 것은 이스라엘에서 처음 보는 일이다."
v.WLB: 그리고 예수께서 귀신을 쫓아내시자 말 못했던 사람이 말을 하게 됐습니다. 사람들은 놀라서 말했습니다. “이스라엘에서 이와 같은 일을 본 적이 없다.”

v.CNV: 但法利赛人说：“他不过是靠鬼王赶鬼罢了。”
v.ESV: But the Pharisees said, “He casts out demons by the prince of demons.”
v.GAE: 바리새인들은 이르되 그가 귀신의 왕을 의지하여 귀신을 쫓아낸다 하더라
v.KJV: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.
v.NASB: But the Pharisees were saying, 'He casts out the demons by the ruler of the demons.'
v.NIV: But the Pharisees said, “It is by the prince of demons that he drives out demons.”
v.NRSV: But the Pharisees said, “By the ruler of the demons he casts out the demons.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 바리새파 사람들은 "그는 귀신의 두목의 힘을 빌어서 귀신을 쫓아낸다" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 바리새파 사람들이 말했습니다. “저가 귀신의 왕을 통해 귀신을 쫓아낸다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣走遍各城各乡，在各会堂里教导人，宣扬天国的福音，医治各种疾病、各种病症。
v.ESV: And Jesus went throughout all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing every disease and every affliction.
v.GAE: 예수께서 모든 도시와 마을에 두루 다니사 그들의 회당에서 가르치시며 천국 복음을 전파하시며 모든 병과 모든 약한 것을 고치시니라
v.KJV: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
v.NASB: Jesus was going through all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and every kind of sickness.
v.NIV: Jesus went through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom and healing every disease and sickness.
v.NRSV: Then Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and curing every disease and every sickness.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 모든 도시와 마을을 두루 다니시면서, 유대 사람의 여러 회당에서 가르치며, 하늘 나라의 복음을 선포하며, 온갖 질병과 온갖 아픔을 고쳐 주셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 모든 도시와 마을을 두루 다니시며 회당에서 가르치시고 하늘나라 복음을 전파하시며 모든 질병과 아픔을 고쳐 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他看见群众，就怜悯他们，因为他们困苦无依，像没有牧人的羊一样。
v.ESV: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
v.GAE: 무리를 보시고 불쌍히 여기시니 이는 그들이 목자 없는 양과 같이 고생하며 기진함이라
v.KJV: But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.
v.NASB: Seeing the people, He felt compassion for them, because they were distressed and dispirited like sheep without a shepherd.
v.NIV: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
v.NRSV: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 무리를 보시고, 그들을 불쌍히 여기셨다. 그들은 마치 목자 없는 양과 같이, 고생에 지쳐서 기운이 빠져 있었기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 무리를 보시고 그들을 불쌍히 여기셨습니다. 그들이 목자 없는 양처럼 시달리고 방황하고 있었기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 他就对门徒说：“庄稼多，工人少；
v.ESV: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
v.GAE: 이에 제자들에게 이르시되 추수할 것은 많되 일꾼이 적으니
v.KJV: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;
v.NASB: Then He said to His disciples, 'The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.
v.NIV: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few.
v.NRSV: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
v.SAENEW: 그래서 제자들에게 말씀하셨다. "추수할 것은 많은데, 일꾼이 적다.
v.WLB: 그때 예수께서 제자들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “추수할 것은 많은데 일꾼이 적구나.

v.CNV: 所以你们应当求庄稼的主派工人去收割。”
v.ESV: therefore pray earnestly to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”
v.GAE: 그러므로 추수하는 주인에게 청하여 추수할 일꾼들을 보내 주소서 하라 하시니라
v.KJV: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.
v.NASB: 'Therefore beseech the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into His harvest.'
v.NIV: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.”
v.NRSV: therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 너희는 추수하는 주인에게 일꾼들을 그의 추수밭으로 보내시라고 청하여라."
v.WLB: 그러므로 추수할 주인에게 추수할 들판으로 일꾼을 보내 달라고 요청하라.”
